prompt: Write Facebook ad copy for my skincare brand. We sell a vitamin C serum for $48. Target is women 30-55 who have tried other serums without seeing results. This is cold traffic. CTA is to buy now. Our differentiator is that we use 20% stabilized L-ascorbic acid — most competitors use 10-15%.
assertions: Asks no clarifying questions — all required info provided
Produces at least 3 headline variants
Produces at least 2 body copy variants
Primary text stays under 2200 chars; flags if near 125 recommended
Headline stays under 40 chars; flags any overage
Description stays under 30 chars
Includes CTA button label from approved Meta list
Each variation includes a Strategic Note with angle, awareness level, platform optimization logic, and objection addressed
At least one hook uses pain/frustration angle (tried other serums, no results)
At least one hook uses proof or differentiator angle (20% vs 10-15%)
Copy names a specific benefit or outcome, not just a feature
Includes risk reversal or guarantee language in at least one variant
Mentions Meta Andromeda optimization in strategic notes (e.g., standalone headline, first 3 words)

prompt: I need Google Search ad copy for my HR software. It automates employee onboarding — companies using it cut onboarding time from 3 weeks to 3 days. We offer a free 30-day trial, no credit card. Target keywords: 'employee onboarding software' and 'hr onboarding tools'. Audience is HR managers at companies with 50-500 employees.
assertions: Produces minimum 3 headlines (ideally 5+), each under 30 chars
Produces minimum 2 descriptions, each under 90 chars
Flags any asset over character limit and provides trimmed version
Includes callout extensions (25 chars max each)
Includes at least one sitelink extension with correct format (headline + 2 description lines)
Headlines are varied in angle — at least one outcome-led, one proof/number-led, one urgency/offer-led
Descriptions include the 3-week to 3-day specific claim
Mentions that each headline must function independently (Google GEM asset combination logic)
Strategic note explains why headline variety enables Google to optimize combinations
Free trial + no credit card appears in copy
Display URL path suggestion included

prompt: Write retargeting ads for Facebook for my business coaching program. These people visited my sales page but didn't buy. Price is $2,997. They've seen our brand before. Offer includes: 12 weeks of group coaching, weekly live calls, a private community, and a 100% money-back guarantee if they don't see results in 90 days.
assertions: Recognizes warm audience and selects Product-Aware or Most-Aware angle
Does not use cold-traffic curiosity hooks — these people know the brand
Prominently features the money-back guarantee (risk reversal)
Value stacks the offer components (coaching, calls, community) to justify $2,997
Includes at least one social proof element or results reference
Includes urgency or scarcity if applicable, or explains why not
Produces 3 headline variants and 2 body copy variants
All field formats and character limits correct for Facebook
Strategic note identifies this as warm retargeting and explains why the angle matches that awareness level
Addresses price objection either through value stacking, guarantee, or ROI framing
